medications.indication_rationale: Lubricates the mouth before/at meals so food slides without adhering; the mainstay of relief.
Soothes irritated mucosa; avoid alcohol-based rinses.
A rheumatology/GP decision for ongoing sicca, not an acute telehealth initiation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Spray artificial saliva 5 minutes before meals, avoid dry/crumbly foods, sip water with meals, and chew sugar-free gum to stimulate saliva.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents food adhering to dry mucosa and eases dysphagia.

patient_education_points.point: Your Sjogren's means too little saliva, so dry crackers stuck and burned — but you can swallow water and saliva, your voice is normal and your lips aren't swollen, so nothing is blocking your throat and this isn't an allergic reaction.
Use artificial saliva before meals, avoid dry/crumbly foods, sip water with food, and chew sugar-free gum. See your GP/rheumatologist about a saliva-stimulating medicine.
Go to ED / call 000 if you become unable to swallow your own saliva, start drooling, your voice changes or you get noisy breathing, or your lips/tongue swell or you struggle to breathe.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Sicca management, secretagogues, and RA overlap therapy.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Inability to swallow saliva/drooling (bolus), or lip/tongue swelling/wheeze (angioedema).

must_include_items: Exclude food-bolus impaction (swallows water/saliva, normal voice, no drooling) and angioedema (no lip/tongue swelling/wheeze)
Advise artificial saliva before meals and avoiding dry foods
Reassure the airway/throat is not blocked and this is not an allergy
errors_of_commission: Ambulance/ED over-triage for a patient swallowing water with a normal voice
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T1/T2 case: passing requires the bolus/angioedema screen + lubrication advice + safety-net. ED over-triage of friction dysphagia with intact swallow/voice is a failure.
